ArchitectureNotes.objective: Multi-user Telegram → Claude Code CLI WIKI assistant (Karpathy LLM Wiki method). MVP delivers: foundation (settings/logging/three-DB SQLite), auth + onboarding + admin, scheduler core, Stage-0 Haiku classifier, Stage-1 Sonnet wiki runner + lifecycle, inbox materialise + idempotency, ops (PII/retention, backup/git), Telegram I/O text layer + voice STT + photo ingest, ingest pipeline (classifier + streaming), runtime wiring, integration E2E.
ArchitectureNotes.non-goal: No i18n catalog (ru-only, D-032); off-site backup deferred; web UI / non-Telegram channels out of scope.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-1: users.toml drift between SSoT file and sessions.db cache — mitigated by SIGHUP+watchdog reload + sha256 short-circuit (D-031).
ArchitectureNotes.risk-2: SQLite three-DB topology requires per-engine PRAGMAs; missed PRAGMA = silent corruption — mitigated by shared apply_sqlite_pragmas listener.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-3: Claude CLI subprocess invocation drift between Stage-0 and Stage-1 (system prompt, cwd, stdin, --verbose) — mitigated by shared M-CLAUDE-CLI-COMMON primitives + integration test against the real CLI.

Phase-0.NAME: Foundation scaffold
Phase-0.description: Repo skeleton, pyproject + uv lock, ruff/mypy, pre-commit, settings, structured logging, three-DB engines + ORM models, users.toml allowlist with SIGHUP/watchdog hot-reload.

Phase-4.NAME: Scheduler core
Phase-4.description: APScheduler AsyncIOScheduler factory (D-003), 5-lane priority queue, 3-tier WIKI lock manager, failure taxonomy + 3-strikes auto-disable (D-019), DLQ insert, SIGTERM→grace→SIGKILL kill sequence (D-021), periodic maintenance-job registration.

Phase-5.NAME: Stage-0 classifier
Phase-5.description: Stage-0 Haiku intent classifier: prompt cache (semver+sha256), backend abstraction (Claude CLI / Anthropic API / Fake), Pydantic intent schema, NL time parser (dateparser → Haiku-fallback → escalate). Hardened by aisw-p5b (CLI envelope) and aisw-d3i (CLI alignment).

Phase-6.NAME: Inbox-WIKI materialise + hint cache
Phase-6.description: Per-user Inbox-WIKI/ first-contact materialise, "## Inbox hint" parser + sessions.inbox_hint_cache repo (stat→cache-hit hot path), media staging+promotion (D-004, D-016, D-022). Extended by aisw-8r9 (promote-on-success + 24h sweep).

Phase-7.NAME: Wiki Stage-1 runner
Phase-7.description: Stage-1a/1b Sonnet runner: assemble prompt (base+overlay+per-WIKI CLAUDE.md), acquire WIKI lock, spawn `claude --output-format stream-json --verbose`, parse stream events, hard timeout + kill-sequence, aggregate assistant text. Hardened by aisw-d3i/aisw-kpb/aisw-w83; aisw-m2m (media paths) + aisw-t0n (per-call vision timeout).

Phase-8.NAME: Wiki lifecycle
Phase-8.description: WIKI name normalisation (Cyrillic→Latin ISO 9, PascalCase), owner-scoped create/lookup/soft-delete/restore with anti-spam cap + near-duplicate detection, CLAUDE.md frontmatter + managed/user-zone markers + template loader, 5-step pre-flight grounding (D-008, D-039, D-041).

Phase-9.NAME: Ingest idempotency
Phase-9.description: Two-layer ingest dedup (D-018): L1 tg_updates (24h) + L2 seen_files (SHA-256, 30d) with NFKC text normalisation; records dedup outcomes into audit.db.

Phase-10.NAME: TG text layer
Phase-10.description: aiogram bot + allowlist outer-middleware (D-031), graduated 3-tier confirmations + 10min TTL (D-023), output-size hybrid policy + persistence (D-024, D-025), HTML-balanced throttled streaming edits (D-026).

Phase-11.NAME: TG media — voice + photo
Phase-11.description: Voice (ogg/opus) faster-whisper CPU STT + staging hand-off; photo ingestion → MediaRef → Claude vision via the wiki pipeline; staging→raw promotion (D-022). audio + video_note route to the voice path (aisw-hcl).

Phase-12.NAME: Onboarding + admin
Phase-12.description: Unknown-telegram_id onboarding (pending_users CRUD + intro template + slug lint), admin surface (approve/reject pending, /admin elevate + 30min token, demote, shadow channel), TENANCY_MODE (D-028, D-030, D-031, D-032).

Phase-13.NAME: Ops — PII + retention + GDPR
Phase-13.description: Tiered PII redactor (NIST SP 800-122) for structlog + DB writes, §10.4 retention purge jobs + _trash/_staging sweeps, GDPR purge_user admin endpoint with allow-listed stores (D-034 §10.4). Wired into __main__ by aisw-7k0.

Phase-14.NAME: Ops — backup + per-WIKI git
Phase-14.description: Daily db_snapshot (VACUUM INTO state/snapshots/<UTC>/, 7d rolling retention), per-WIKI local git auto-commit helpers, restore runbook (D-037, tech-spec §10.2/§10.5).

Phase-18.NAME: Runtime wiring
Phase-18.description: Process entrypoint (__main__.py) composing existing modules into a runnable bot: Settings → per-DB alembic upgrade → engines → allowlist sync → APScheduler → aiogram polling → graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown. Enables `uv run python -m ai_steward_wiki` locally with AISW_TG_BOT_TOKEN_LOCAL. No production handlers yet; unrouted updates silently ignored (separate future chunk).

Phase-19.NAME: TG handlers wiring
Phase-19.description: Compose aiogram Router with 5 handlers (text/voice/photo/document/confirm callback) wired into Dispatcher via build_dispatcher(pipeline=...). DefaultPipeline coordinates L1 idempotency dedup, optional voice/photo staging, ack delivery via TgSender, and confirmation callback resolution. Building blocks unchanged; this chunk is composition + e2e tests.

Phase-20.NAME: TG pipeline classifier wiring
Phase-20.description: Replace ack-only DefaultPipeline.on_text/on_voice with Stage-0 Haiku Classifier → Inbox.stage (L2 dedup) → WikiRunner (Stage-1a/1b Sonnet) → deliver_output. Closes the core MVP-launch gap. SSoT: docs/superpowers/plans/20260511-ai-steward-wiki-launch/breakdown.xml chunk-20. bd_id materializes via bd create at chunk start.

Phase-21.NAME: TG pipeline streaming
Phase-21.description: Wrap WikiRunner with StreamEditor (1.5s throttle, Δ50, chain-split 4000) when estimated_duration_s > 5 (DEC-L2). Placeholder edit-in-place + final flush guarantee. SSoT: launch breakdown chunk-21.

Phase-22.NAME: TG document full handler
Phase-22.description: Mime-routing in on_document (DEC-L3): pdf→OCR, text/*→inline, image/*→photo path, else reject. L2 dedup on doc_sha256. Filename PII tier-2 hash. SSoT: launch breakdown chunk-22.

Phase-23.NAME: Integration E2E suite
Phase-23.description: tests/integration/test_e2e_pipeline.py against real Claude CLI + FakeAiogramBot. 4 scenarios: text, voice, photo+confirm, pdf. make integration target. SSoT: launch breakdown chunk-23. Last gate before production cutover (see cutover-checklist.md).

Phase-A.NAME: Inbox-WIKI Phase-A — stage raw content + Router-Claude invocation
Phase-A.description: First slice of smart-inbox-routing (epic aisw-t2r). New inbox/router.py (RouterIntent, RouterDecision, RouterError, parse_router_reply); prompts/inbox.md tightened to a fenced ```router block (v1.1.0); DefaultPipeline gains an optional Router — routable intents (WIKI_INGEST/WIKI_QUERY/UNKNOWN) go through router.route(), the bot replies with RouterDecision.notes; _RouterAdapter in __main__ (ensure_inbox_wiki → stage raw payload into Inbox-WIKI/raw/ → run_wiki_session in Inbox-WIKI/ with prompts/inbox.md → parse_router_reply; WikiRunnerError→RouterError). Domain-WIKI move + Stage-1b ingest + confirm loop + cron bridge + hint fast-path are later phases (aisw-zd9/e45/kcz/12t). NOTE: do not deploy master with Phase-A until aisw-zd9 closes. Discovery/design/plan: docs/superpowers/specs|plans/20260512-inbox-wiki-router-*.md.

Phase-B2.NAME: Inbox-WIKI Phase-B — RouterDecision → domain WIKI select/materialise + move + Stage-1b ingest
Phase-B2.description: Closes the Phase-A loop (epic aisw-t2r). New inbox/route.py (RouteTarget/RouteRejection/StagedRaw; resolve_target_wiki — lookup-or-create the target <Domain>-WIKI, ROUTE-missing → create + warning, AntiSpamCapError/WikiNameError → rejection; stage_raw_into_wiki — write raw/<ts>_<source>.md + promote media via promote_path_to_raw; pick_domain_overlay; build_ingest_prompt). DefaultPipeline gains an optional Librarian + IngestOutcome — a ROUTE/CREATE_WIKI RouterDecision (with a wired librarian + output) → librarian.ingest() → reply notes + Stage-1b summary via OutputDelivery (ok) or send_message (rejected/run_failed); CLARIFY/REJECT and librarian=None keep Phase-A behaviour. _LibrarianAdapter in __main__ (resolve target → stage raw → run_wiki_session in <Domain>-WIKI/ with prompts/wiki.md + domain overlay, timeout_s=None → IngestOutcome). Confirm loop + cron bridge + hint fast-path remain later (aisw-e45/kcz/12t). With Phase-A+B done the "do not deploy master" caveat is lifted. Discovery/design/plan: docs/superpowers/specs|plans/20260512-inbox-wiki-route-ingest-*.md.

Phase-C.NAME: Inbox-WIKI Phase-C — route confirm loop (propose via inline buttons, execute on confirm)
Phase-C.description: Adds a one-tap user confirmation between the Stage-1a router decision and the Stage-1b move+ingest (epic aisw-t2r). DefaultPipeline._run_text_pipeline no longer ingests a ROUTE/CREATE_WIKI RouterDecision immediately — it builds a route_ingest confirm draft (RouterDecision + user_text + source + media_paths + correlation_id, via inbox.route.route_action_to_payload) and proposes it through ConfirmationService.request_explicit with a 2-button keyboard (tg.confirm.build_route_confirm_keyboard); on_confirm_callback dispatches route_ingest pending rows to _handle_route_confirm — resolve race-safely, on "confirmed" replay the decision through Librarian.ingest (the Phase-B path) + deliver the reply, else send a ru cancelled/stale notice; cancel/TTL leaves the staged raw in Inbox (D-022). Reuses pending_confirms (no migration); no proposed_actions field on RouterDecision; no toggle (replace, not flag). New inbox/route.py RouteAction + route_action_to/from_payload; new tg/confirm.py build_route_confirm_keyboard + request_explicit keyboard_factory; new tg/pipeline.py ROUTE_CONFIRM_* constants + build_route_recap + _handle_route_confirm; new anchors tg.pipeline.route.confirm_requested|confirm_executed|confirm_cancelled|confirm_stale + tg.pipeline.confirm.route_dispatched. ADR-005. Cron bridge + hint fast-path remain later (aisw-kcz/12t). Discovery/design/plan: docs/superpowers/specs|plans/20260512-inbox-wiki-route-confirm-*.md.
